economic_notes: First Nation operates multiple commercial enterprises including Tobacco Fund, Fisheries, Gas Bar, VLT, Bayside Development Corporation, and Bayside Operations. Also holds investments in E'sukutimkewey Limited Partnership and Mi'kmaq Resource Development Partnership with 12 other NS First Nations. Holds a 6.14% promissory note receivable from First Nations Fisheries Interest Group Partnership of $34,662,230 maturing 2051.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Province is Nova Scotia. The statement of financial position page was not included in the provided text so tangible capital assets net book value could not be extracted. Net financial liabilities (net debt) of $3,138,589 shown as negative. ISC and total government transfers breakdown not separately disclosed in the excerpt provided (Note 15 Government Transfers page appears blank). Restricted cash consists of replacement reserve fund $482,935 and debt reserve fund $2,742,049. Large promissory note receivable from First Nations Fisheries Interest Group of $34.66M is a significant non-standard financial asset. CMHC housing expenses are a combined $180,829 for both projects. Expenses_enterprises includes Tobacco Fund $1,640,645, Fisheries $1,174,235, VLT $588,438, Bayside Development $1,157,340, Bayside Operations $10,952,650 less Economic Development and Native Employment $1,478,342 which is reported separately. Remuneration schedule not included in provided text.
